text.title: gce-instance-templates Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for gce-instance-templates as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using a specific image version instead of an image family in the instance template.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pinned images never receive OS security patches automatically. New instances created months later will boot a stale, potentially vulnerable image. Image families resolve to the latest patched image at creation time with zero template changes.

failure-mode.description: Reusing the default Compute Engine service account for application VMs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The default SA has project editor permissions. Any VM compromise yields full project access. Create a dedicated SA and assign only the IAM roles the workload actually needs.

failure-mode.description: Hardcoding configuration values in the startup script instead of reading from instance metadata.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hardcoded values make the template environment-specific, defeating its reusability. Use instance metadata keys for environment, version, and config — the same template can then serve dev, staging, and production by varying only the metadata at MIG creation time.
